summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts`: the test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expected the registry URL `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (without a trailing slash) but `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with a trailing slash). This caused the entire test suite to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command returns a registry URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expects the URL without a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). The `toBe` equality check fails because of this trailing slash difference.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the exact failing test suite and test name.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: expected no trailing slash, received a trailing slash.
Shows the test assertion at line 32 of test/config/get.ts that fails due to the trailing slash.
Pinpoints the exact file and line number of the failing assertion.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly 1 test failed, which is the one identified above.
Shows the test failure propagated to exit code 1, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Fix the `pnpm config get` command to strip the trailing slash from registry URLs when outputting them, or update the test at `test/config/get.ts:32` to accept a trailing slash (e.g., use `trimEnd('/')` before the assertion or change the expected value to include the trailing slash).
